title: Prim, Kruskal, Wilson, Bias, and Distribution Claims · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed puzzle review reconstructs prim, kruskal, wilson, bias, and distribution claims from revision, configuration, seed, topology, frontier, predecessor map, state transition, draw record, accessibility report, profile, artifact, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for comparative seeded generation laboratory after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.
Shuffle candidate walls and use disjoint sets to admit only edges joining different components.
Construct a uniform spanning tree with loop-erased random walks and state termination assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Generator Selection, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Generator Selection precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Generator Selection, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A review puzzle team handles case 9776 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare multiple generators across seeded topology and bias evidence without unsupported distribution claims; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Generator Selection, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: The fastest generator is automatically the best game design.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using select and generator plus specific case evidence.
